description: Rust language ergonomics/safety helpers (tasks 209-216) and Base Azul-oriented review heuristics (217-223) on the shared .rs adapter.

rules.title: Unsafe block or attribute without SAFETY commentary
rules.summary: Require // SAFETY: before unsafe blocks and #[unsafe(...)] uses (skip cfg(test)).

rules.title: Borrow of static mut binding
rules.summary: Flag references to identifiers declared via static mut.
rules.graph_hint: edge from function to state static_mut

rules.title: Interior mutability constructs near spawning APIs
rules.summary: Heuristic coupling of RefCell/Cell types with threading entrypoints.
rules.graph_hint: compound type lacks Sync trait binding

rules.title: panic!, unwrap, expect exposed publicly
rules.summary: Prefer Result over traps in externally visible functions (skips cfg(test) attrs).

rules.title: Arc/Rc clones after loop anchors
rules.summary: Cheap positional heuristic for clones following for/while headers.

rules.title: Legacy async Fn ergonomics audit
rules.summary: Suggests migration toward AsyncFn* when Fn+Future footprints appear.

rules.title: Suspicious borrowing of temporaries
rules.summary: Greps let bindings that borrow unnamed temporaries outright.

rules.title: std:: paths under crate no_std
rules.summary: Blocks std references when#![no_std] is declared (respects cfg-gated helpers).

rules.title: Non-deterministic collections in state-root or commitment paths
rules.summary: HashMap/HashSet iteration order can fork Merkle/state derivations; prefer BTreeMap or sorted serialization.
rules.trigger: HashMap or HashSet from std appears alongside state-root/Merkle/commitment-style logic.
rules.rationale: Any iteration-order nondeterminism breaks cross-node state hashes in EL stacks derived from Reth-style execution.
rules.actionable_check: Replace with BTreeMap or sort keys before hashing; audit trie/commitment builders.
rules.graph_hint: collection type participates in hashing pipeline

rules.title: Panic or unwrap leaking across async spawn boundaries
rules.summary: unwrap/expect/panic! near tokio/task spawn risks isolated task failure and partial EL/batcher state.
rules.trigger: tokio::spawn or task::spawn shares a region with unwrap/expect/panic!.
rules.rationale: Detached tasks may panic without tearing down the whole node, leaving half-committed pipeline state.
rules.actionable_check: Propagate Result across spawn boundaries; consider catch_unwind at service edges where justified.
rules.graph_hint: async task boundary without structured error propagation

rules.title: Serde schema drift risk between CL and EL commitments
rules.summary: serde(flatten) or unchecked derive layouts can diverge across base-consensus vs base-reth-node crates.
rules.rationale: Binary interpretation mismatch breaks block/proposal verification between CL and EL.
rules.actionable_check: Align canonical layouts; compare structs across crates; prefer explicit field order/repr where needed.

rules.title: Fork-choice or finality constants misaligned across EL and CL
rules.summary: EL safety vs CL confirmation thresholds must match (distance/finality constants).
rules.trigger: Fork-choice structs/helpers appear without evidence of shared constant modules.
rules.rationale: Earlier EL safety than CL risks building on heads CL later reorgs.
rules.actionable_check: Diff fork-choice rules and MAX_SAFE/FINALITY-style constants across base-consensus and base-reth-node.

rules.title: Gas or execution env mismatch between batching and EL execution
rules.summary: Different Env/basefee/gas caps between batcher simulation and Reth executor halt the chain.
rules.trigger: gas_limit tuning appears alongside batcher-style paths without shared Env helpers.
rules.rationale: Accepted txs that EL rejects stall derivation or sequencing.
rules.actionable_check: Ensure batcher and executor share the same env builder and limit checks.

rules.title: Proposal block ranges missing sane bounds
rules.summary: Huge proposal ranges can DoS nodes loading state or proofs.
rules.trigger: Proposal-like u64 start/end fields without checked span validation.
rules.rationale: Attackers can force OOM or excessive IO during range replay.
rules.actionable_check: Enforce end>=start, span caps vs MAX_BATCH_SIZE, resource budgets on ingress proposals.

rules.title: Distinct panic paths in TEE signing flows
rules.summary: Mixing panic_with_error-style macros with plain panic! may leak timing/inference signals.
rules.trigger: panic_with_error and panic! both appear (Rust-side signing helpers).
rules.rationale: Different error paths can enable observable side channels against signing secrecy goals.
rules.actionable_check: Unify error handling; prefer constant-time comparisons on secrets where relevant.

review_scenarios.prompt: Can an old intermediate commitment be replayed if sequence numbers are outside the signed digest?
If hardfork activates at block N but a depth-1 reorg replaces N with N', does Reth activation logic stay deterministic?
If CL requests state base-reth-node already pruned or archived, does recovery or error handling remain safe and deterministic?

review_checklist: Hash pipelines use only deterministic iteration orders for commitments/state roots.
Every ingress batch or proposal enforces CPU/memory-friendly bounds.
Unsafe blocks keep // SAFETY: notes and preserve EL invariants.
Derivation logic avoids SystemTime::now(); prefer ledger/consensus timestamps from Env.
